incident_report:
# Incident Report: Dungeon Agent Run (Seed 19)

**Summary:** Agents A and B failed to complete the dungeon objective within the 50-turn limit, achieving only 33% progress despite Agent B successfully acquiring the key on turn 2. The run was severely hampered by a systematic environmental anomaly: spurious block obstacles appeared 11 times across 41 turns, predominantly triggered during movement commands.

**Critical Failure Point:** While Agent B obtained the key early (turn 2), no door was ever unlocked, indicating the agents either never reached the target door or were unable to coordinate effectively to use the key. The lack of inter-agent communication (0 messages sent) may have been a contributing factor—neither agent attempted to coordinate their movements or share information about the anomalies.

**Notable Decision Points:** The most significant issue was the agents' apparent inability to develop an adaptive strategy around the recurring spurious blocks. Starting at turn 10, anomalies appeared with increasing frequency in the second half of the run (6 of 11 anomalies occurred between turns 37–50), yet the agents continued attempting direct movement without apparent course correction, routing adjustments, or attempts to contact the DM (0 interactions). The failure to utilize available communication and DM escalation mechanics suggests the agents lacked reactive problem-solving when faced with persistent environmental obstacles.
